data.title: Microsoft expands AI tools for healthcare providers
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) unveiled several new artificial intelligence-powered tools for healthcare providers today through its Microsoft Cloud for Healthcare platform.

These include new healthcare AI models through Azure AI Studio, data management and security in Microsoft Fabric, healthcare AI agents through Copilot Studio and AI-driven solutions for nursing workflows.

The new AI models are expected to handle tasks ranging from genomics to analyzing clinical records.

"The development of foundational AI models in pathology and medical imaging is expected to drive significant advancements in cancer research and diagnostics," said Carlo Bifulco, MD, chief medical officer of Providence Genomics.

Microsoft also launched a new healthcare data management system through Microsoft Fabric, which is available for public preview, the company said.

Meanwhile, healthcare AI agents have already been utilized by hospitals such as the Cleveland Clinic. These agents are intended to automate administrative tasks and analyze data.

Other cloud software companies are getting more involved in healthcare, including Salesforce (CRM), whose latest offering Agentforce, also provides pre-built and customizable AI agents for the healthcare industry.

Finally, Microsoft is also releasing AI technology to relieve some of the burden on nurses, especially in the face of expected workforce shortages. The World Health Organization expects a shortage of 4.5M nurses and 300,000 midwives by 2030.

"AI is transforming nursing workflows by streamlining administrative tasks, allowing nurses to focus more on patient care," said Corey Miller, vice president of R&D at Epic.

"It allows us to focus more on patient care rather than the administrative burden of documentation," added Terry McDonnell the chief nurse executive at Duke University Health System.

data.title: Cyberattacks are on the rise in the wake of a digitalized world fueled by AI
data.description:
Bank of America’s global research team spotlighted that cyberattacks are becoming more and more prevalent with the rise of digitalization and the expansion of artificial intelligence (AI). Breaches are increasing across all areas of the market and the world, as every company at some level is now becoming a data company.

“In 2023, about 6 in 10 organizations were hit by ransomware and the average payment was up by 500% YoY. The global average cost of a data breach in 2024 has risen by 10% YoY to $4.9mn – the highest ever. The Healthcare and Financial sectors continue to suffer the costliest data breaches,” BofA said in an investor note on Thursday.

The institution added: “If cybercrime damage were a state, it would be the world’s third-largest economy.”

See the visual chart below by BofA highlighting the areas that are most and least affected by cyber related attacks in 2023 and 2024:

For investors that are looking to further track moves around the complete cybersecurity industry of the economy, they may choose to further analyze both cybersecurity-based stocks and exchange-traded funds for further details. Outlined below are some popular names worth mentioning.

Cybersecurity Stocks: Palo Alto Networks (PANW), CrowdStrike Holdings (CRWD), Fortinet (FTNT), Check Point Software Technologies (CHKP), Gen Digital (GEN), Okta (OKTA), and Zscaler (ZS).

Cybersecurity ETFs: (CIBR), (HACK), (IHAK), (NASDAQ:BUG), (WCBR), (UCYB), and (SPAM).

data.title: Seres Therapeutics gains amid takeover speculation
data.description:
Seres Therapeutics (NASDAQ:MCRB) rose 5% amid a report that Nestle (OTCPK:NSRGY) has raised its offer for the company.

Nestle (OTCPK:NSRGY) is speculated to have made a new approach for Seres (MCRB) after it's originally offer was rebuffed in the summer, according to traders, who cited a Betaville "rate" alert that circulated on Thursday, which cited people following the matter.

Nestle (OTCPK:NSRGY) is believed to have raised its bid to $6.90 to $7.20 a share for Seres (MCRB), up from the $6 to $6.50 Nestle offered earlier.

The update from Betaville comes after Seres (MCRB) announced in August that Nestlé Health Science, a unit of consumer care giant Nestlé (OTCPK:NSRGY), agreed to purchase its oral microbiome therapy Vowst for $175 million.

Seres (MCRB) short interest is 11.3%.

data.title: Tesla tops list of most heavily shorted securities in September, Hazeltree says
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) regained its position as the most heavily shorted security in the Americas in September, according to the latest Shortside Crowdedness Report from Hazeltree.

The Austin, Texas-based EV maker topped the list as the most crowded security in the large-cap category with a score of 99, moving up from second position in August (97). The Hazeltree Crowdedness Score represents securities that are being shorted by the highest percentage of funds in Hazeltree’s community.

As of Sep 30, traders sold 74.33M shares of TSLA short, representing 2.33% of the float.

“It is notable to see that Tesla regained its position as the top shorted large-cap stock in the Americas, a position which, until July, it had held for nearly every month since we’ve launched the report,” said Tim Smith, managing director of data insights at Hazeltree.

On Semiconductor (NASDAQ:ON) was a close second, followed by Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) and Chevron (NYSE:CVX), all of which scored 96. Chevron (CVX), which had been the most crowded security in this category in August, dropped to the fourth spot this time.

Tractor Supply Company (TSCO) had the highest institutional supply utilization (12.83%) - an indicator of how “hot” a security is in terms of the supply demand dynamic. Tractor Supply (TSCO) had a short interest of 7M shares as of Sep 30, representing 6.51% of the float.

In the mid-cap category, Bloom Energy (BE) and Shift4 Payments (FOUR) were the most crowded securities, each with a Hazeltree Crowdedness Score of 99. Wolfspeed (WOLF) continued its hold as the most crowded security in the small-cap category.

These were the top 10 most shorted large-cap securities in the Americas in September, according to Hazeltree:

#1 Tesla (TSLA): Hazeltree Crowdedness Score of 99

#2 On Semiconductor (ON): Hazeltree Crowdedness Score of 96

#3 Western Digital (WDC): Hazeltree Crowdedness Score of 96

#4 Chevron (CVX): Hazeltree Crowdedness Score of 96

#5 Caterpillar (CAT): Hazeltree Crowdedness Score of 93

#6 Deere &Company (DE): Hazeltree Crowdedness Score of 93

#7 Live Nation Entertainment (LYV): Hazeltree Crowdedness Score of 89

#8 IBM (IBM): Hazeltree Crowdedness Score of 89

#9 Church & Dwight (CHD): Hazeltree Crowdedness Score of 86

#10 Tractor Supply Company (TSCO): Hazeltree Crowdedness Score of 86

data.title: Pfizer reports positive Phase 3 results for Talzenna/Xtandi combo
data.description:
• Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) said topline results from a Phase 3 study showed that Talzenna in combination with Xtandi was able to significantly prolong overall survival in patients with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer, or mCRPC, regardless of mutation status.
  • The study, called TALAPRO-2, evaluated Talzenna in combination with Xtandi versus Xtandi alone. Pfizer said it plans to share the results with global regulators to potentially update Talzenna’s label, according to a statement.
  • Talzenna in combination with Xtandi is already approved by the FDA for the treatment of HRR gene-mutated mCRPC. The combination is also approved by EU regulators for the treatment of mCRPC in whom chemotherapy is not clinically indicated.
  • Xtandi is marketed by Astellas (OTCPK:ALPMY) (OTCPK:ALPMF) and Pfizer (PFE), while Talzenna is marketed by Pfizer (PFE).

data.title: Biotech and Retail ETFs have the highest implied call return according to Goldman
data.description:
Biotechnology ETFs (NYSEARCA:XBI), (IBB) have the most elevated upside relative to January 2025 call prices, based on single stock price targets, according to Goldman Sachs analysts.

When comparing the weighted average single stock price targets of ETFs to January 2025 call prices to identify attractive risk-adjusted upside opportunities, biotech (XBI), (IBB), and retail (XRT) ETFs see the highest returns.

“For example, our analysts cover 83% of the weight of the iShares NASDAQ Biotechnology ETF (IBB) and have a weighted average upside to price target of +25.3%; this implies a return of 406% on buying an IBB Jan-2025 at-the-money call for 5.0%,” said John Marshall, head of derivatives research at Goldman, in a note.

The upside implied by price targets is the lowest relative to the cost of options for utilities (XLU), semiconductors (SOXX), and energy (XLE).

To achieve these results, analysts calculate the sum of the weight of each stock in each index, multiply by the upside to price targets, and then divide by the total weight covered by Goldman analysts.

Here is the weighted average valuation for major ETFs and their implied returns on buying January 2025 at-the-money calls if ETFs reach their implied valuation:

  • SPDR S&P Biotech (XBI) – Implied weighted upside: 37.2%; Cost of call: 6.1%; Implied call return: 513%
  • iShares NASDAQ Biotechnology (IBB) – Implied weighted upside: 25.3%; Cost of call: 5.0%; Implied call return: 406%
  • SPDR S&P Retail (XRT) – Implied weighted upside: 14.6%; Cost of call: 5.4%; Implied call return: 171%
  • Materials SPDR (XLB) – Implied weighted upside: 10.6%; Cost of call: 4.0%; Implied call return: 166%
  • Vanguard Total Stock Market (VTI) – Implied weighted upside: 10.3%; Cost of call: 3.9%; Implied call return: 162%
  • SPDR S&P 500 (SPY) – Implied weighted upside: 10.1%; Cost of call: 3.9%; Implied call return: 157%
  • Consumer Staples SPDR (XLP) – Implied weighted upside: 7.7%; Cost of call: 3.1%; Implied call return: 145%
  • Invesco QQQ (QQQ) – Implied weighted upside: 11.9%; Cost of call: 5.0%; Implied call return: 139%
  • Health Care SPDR (XLV) – Implied weighted upside: 8.1%; Cost of call: 3.4%; Implied call return: 136%
  • SPDR S&P Oil and Gas E&P (XOP) – Implied weighted upside: 15.1%; Cost of call: 6.6%; Implied call return: 128%
  • Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight (RSP) – Implied weighted upside: 8.5%; Cost of call: 3.8%; Implied call return: 127%
  • Financial SPDR (XLF) – Implied weighted upside: 8.9%; Cost of call: 4.1%; Implied call return: 115%
  • Communication Services SPDR (XLC) – Implied weighted upside: 8.9%; Cost of call: 4.2%; Implied call return: 113%
  • Technology Select Sector SPDR (XLK) – Implied weighted upside: 11.1%; Cost of call: 5.6%; Implied call return: 100%
  • Consumer Discretionary (XLY) – Implied weighted upside: 9.0%; Cost of call: 4.9%; Implied call return: 85%

data.title: Sen. Elizabeth Warrens wants FTC to scrutinize Novo Nordisk deal for Catalent
data.description:
Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) is calling on the Federal Trade Commission to carefully evaluate Novo Nordisk Foundation's planned $16.5 billion acquisition of Catalent (NYSE:CTLT).

"I am concerned that this deal could increase Novo Nordisk’s dominance2 over vital GLP-1 inhibitor drugs, reducing competition and increasing prices for patients," Warren wrote in a letter to FTC Chair Lina Khan on Wednesday.

Catalent (CTLT) and Novo Nordisk Foundation received a request for more information from the FTC in May on their planned deal. In February, Catalent said it had agreed to be acquired by Novo Holdings for $63.50 a share. Novo Holdings will then sell three Catalent manufacturing sites to Novo Nordisk (NVO), which should help increase production of the company’s hugely popular weight-loss drug, Wegovy.

"I am concerned that Novo Nordisk’s merger with Catalent will give Novo Nordisk unprecedented visibility into and control over its competitor’s production capacity, costs, and business practices, and the ability to preference its own products and obstruct its competitors’ use of Catalent to produce GLP-1 drugs," Warren added in the letter.

"Given the proposed merger’s potential to further limit competition in the weight loss and diabetes drug markets, making it harder for patients to access these drugs, I urge FTC to closely scrutinize this acquisition and block any activity found to be illegal under antitrust law," Warren wrote.

Shares of Catalent (CTLT) ticked lower by 0.5% on Thursday.

data.title: Nutrien downgraded at UBS on weakening agriculture demand
data.description:
Nutrien (NRT) on Thursday was downgraded to Neutral from a previous investment rating of Buy among analysts at financial-services firm UBS. They said they maker of farming chemicals faces a weakening agriculture market that limits demand.

“We previously believed Nutrien’s (NYSE:NTR) stock could re-rate back towards historical levels as potash markets stabilized,” Joshua Spector, analyst at UBS, said in an October 10 report. “However, when we look at our latest earnings forecast and run that through to free cash flow, we see a less compelling upside scenario.”

UBS lowered its price target on Nutrien (NTR) to $51 a share from $66 a share previously, based on an estimated enterprise value that’s about 6.5 times earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization.

data.title: NY Fed's Williams sees more rate cuts ahead in wake of inflation progress
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said Thursday he sees additional interest-rate cuts ahead, amid the central bank's progress in reducing inflation.

“Based on my current forecast for the economy, I expect that it will be appropriate to continue the process of moving the stance of monetary policy to a more neutral setting over time,” Williams said in remarks prepared for delivery at Binghamton University.

While there's still a gap between where the inflation rate currently stands and the Fed's 2% goal, "we’re definitely moving in the right direction.”

The September Consumer Price Index report, released earlier on Thursday, signaled that inflation risks still remain a concern, with both the headline number and the core figure coming in hotter than expected on a monthly basis.

The pace and size of future rate reductions will continue to be "based on the evolution of the data, the economic outlook, and the risks to achieving our goals,” Williams said.

The New York Fed chief noted "the economy is in a good place," and "the risks to achieving" the Fed's dual mandate of stable prices and full employment are "now in balance."

He expects the economy to expand by 2.25%-2.50% this year and to average +2.25% over the next two years. He sees unemployment rising to 4.25% by the end of 2024 and staying there in the following year. PCE inflation, in his view, is expected to ease to 2.25% this year and move close to the 2% objective next year.

data.title: Synovus, UMB Financial get new Buy ratings at Citi in a look at regional banks
data.description:
Citi initiated coverage of four regional banks that have attracted more investor interest after recent M&A transactions with Buy ratings on Synovus Financial (NYSE:SNV) and UMB Financial (NASDAQ:UMBF).

Analyst Benjamin Gerlinger assigned Neutral ratings to Banc of California (NYSE:BANC) and SouthState (NYSE:SSB).

While Synovus (SNV) is likely to track the national trend of "somewhat sluggish" loan growth in Q3, its lending hires branch footprint boasting higher than the national average GDP outlook should generate multiyear loan growth that outpaces its peers.

Gerlinger sees "substantial opportunities" after the company's acquisition of Heartland Financial (HTLF) to generate market-leading return on tangible common equity and to capture market share.

In addition, "we believe the combination of these two balance sheets is being overly discounted by consensus expectations," the analyst wrote in a note to clients.

Meanwhile, Banc of California's (BANC) liability-sensitive balance sheet modest valuation premium and compressing expense outlook already appears to be priced into its valuation.

SouthState's (SSB) premium valuation leaves little room for further multiple expansion near term. Citi prefers to wait for a more attractive entry point.

However, the SA Quant rating prefers SouthState (SSB) over Synovus (SNV) and UMB Financial (UMBF). The SA Quant system has no rating on Banc of California (BANC).

In Thursday morning trading, Synovus (SNV) stock rose 0.7%, UMB Financial (UMBF) was little changed, Banc of California (BANC) dropped 1.3%, and SouthState (SSB) fell 0.7%.
